source_jobs.title: Commercial Sales Manager
Defense Business Development Manager
source_jobs.description: <h2>What we're building</h2> <p>The marine industry supports recreation, global commerce, and national defense. And yet it runs on technology that hasn't meaningfully changed in fifty years. Until now.</p> <p>Arc is on a mission to electrify the marine industry. We're rethinking powertrains, software, and vessel designs from first principles. And we're already executing. We started with consumers to develop and harden our core technology. We're now scaling production of our flagship model with new models and sectors on the horizon. Now we're extending our platform to commercial applications, starting with tugs, ferries, and defense, with years worth of signed contracts and a pipeline that spans every vessel type on the water.</p> <p>The technology is proven, the momentum is building, and the opportunity is enormous. The marine industry is going electric and Arc is building the platform that powers it.</p> <h2>Who we're looking for</h2> <p>As Arc’s Defense Business Development Manager, you will own identifying, cultivating, and leading high-impact partnerships across the defense, technology, and maritime sectors.</p> <p>We're looking for someone fired up by the mission and ready to build. You'll thrive here if you move fast, take ownership, and care about the details.</p> <p>This is a full-time, on-site role at our Los Angeles headquarters. You'll report to our CTO and work closely with a lean, high-trust team where everyone’s work matters.</p> <h2>What you'll do</h2> <ul> <li>Developing and executing government contracting and sales strategies across the Navy, defense primes, and adjacent government entities</li> <li>Identifying and prioritizing opportunities across defense and commercial maritime sectors with particular focus on autonomous and unmanned platforms</li> <li>Engaging senior stakeholders from defense primes, government entities, and the military</li> <li>Identifying relevant programs and contracts where our technology can be applied, with a strong understanding of defense procurement pathways</li> <li>Lead and execute complex program capture strategies for programs</li> <li>Mapping relevant programs and contract vehicles to Arc's powertrain and power distribution capabilities, including emerging vehicles like Golden Fleet</li> <li>Guiding business strategy using market research, advisor networks, and a command of the broader competitive landscape</li> </ul> <h2>Minimum Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>Bachelor's degree in business, engineering, or a related field; MBA or advanced degree preferred</li> <li>7+ years of experience in a strategic role in a high-velocity environment, with exposure to defense, maritime, aerospace, or robotics industries</li> <li>Demonstrated track record of closing deals, winning programs, and forming high-value partnerships with public and private sector entities</li> <li>Strong understanding of defense procurement pathways and the Defense Industrial Base</li> <li>Eligible for security clearance as needed</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to present complex ideas clearly and concisely</li> <li>Comfortable operating in ambiguity and building a function from scratch at a fast but sustainable pace</li> <li>Ability to travel regularly (approximately 20–30%)</li> </ul> <h2>Bonus Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>Experience working at an emerging technology defense contractor</li> <li>Experience in strategy consulting</li> <li>Experience in the maritime industry or with a government agency dealing with the marine domain</li> <li>D

Electrical Integration Engineer

And yet it runs on technology that hasn't meaningfully changed in fifty years. Until now.</p> <p>Arc is on a mission to electrify the marine industry. Now we're extending our platform to commercial applications, starting with tugs, ferries, and defense, with years worth of signed contracts and a pipeline that spans every vessel type on the water.</p> <p>The technology is proven, the momentum is building, and the opportunity is enormous. The marine industry is going electric and Arc is building the platform that powers it.</p> <h2>Who we're looking for</h2> <p>As Arc’s Electrical Integration Engineer, you will be solving production problems, improving processes, supporting technicians on the floor, and creating robust electrical systems that scale into volume manufacturing.</p> <p>You’ll thrive here if you move quickly, take ownership, communicate clearly, and enjoy turning complex problems into practical solutions.</p> <p>We're looking for someone fired up by the mission and ready to build.</p> <p>This is a full-time, on-site role at our Los Angeles headquarters. You'll report to the Manager of Electrical Engineering and work closely with Electrical Engineering, Manufacturing Engineering, Supply Chain, Quality, and Production teams.</p> <h2>What you'll do</h2> <ul> <li>Supporting diagnosis of issues through hands on electrical testing and over-the-air data acquisition, collaborating with software and controls engineers</li> <li>Supporting production teams daily by troubleshooting harnesses, electrical integration, and build issues on the manufacturing floor</li> <li>Driving root cause analysis and corrective actions for recurring production and field issues</li> <li>Driving constant improvement of full vehicle electrical system through manufacturing, design, validation, and process improvement</li> <li>Partnering with technicians and assemblers to optimize installation methods, routing paths, tooling, and work instructions</li> <li>Coordinating with outsourced harness suppliers on design intent, build quality, ECNs, first articles, and production readiness</li> <li>Supporting pilot builds, line launches, process validation, and production ramp activities</li> <li>Developing and improving test procedures for harness validation, continuity, hipot, and end-of-line verification</li> <li>Contributing to production documentation including build instructions, torque specs, inspection standards, and repair procedures</li> <li>Supporting vehicle electrical integration in a fast-paced production environment with urgency and high attention to detail</li> </ul> <h2>Minimum Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>Experience in marine, automotive, or EV vehicle production environments</li> <li>Experience supporting production environments and resolving line-down or build-impacting issues quickly</li> <li>Experience interfacing with electrical schematics, formboards, drawings, and engineering change releases</li> <li>Hands-on experience building, reworking, or repairing harnesses</li> <li>Experience selecting connectors, terminals, crimps, seals, wire types, and tooling</li> <li>Familiarity with vehicle electrical systems and communication protocols (CAN, LIN, Ethernet, etc.)</li> <li>Strong

revenue growth by identifying, qualifying, and closing new business opportunities within marina operators, charter fleets, boat dealerships, and direct consumer segments</li> <li>Managing the full technology, battery systems, and software platform into clear, differentiated value propositions for diverse customer profiles</li> <li>Building and maintaining deep, trust-based relationships with key customers, channel partners, and marine industry stakeholders to maximize long-term account growth and retention</li> <li>Collaborating cross-functionally with Engineering, Product, and Manufacturing to capture customer requirements, inform product roadmap decisions, and ensure successful vessel delivery</li> <li>Developing and executing strategic account plans that align customer goals with Arc’s commercial objectives and market expansion strategy</li> <li>Monitoring market dynamics including competitor activity, marine electrification trends, regulatory developments (e.g., emission-free zone mandates), and emerging customer needs</li> <li>Preparing technical proposals, bid packages, and sales presentations that accurately reflect Arc’s capabilities, specs, and integration requirements</li> <li>Owning customer satisfaction from pre-sale through commissioning, serving as the primary escalation point for commercial and technical issues</li> <li>Providing market intelligence and sales forecasting to inform Long Range Planning, product pricing strategy, and go-to-market decisions</li> </ul> <h2>Minimum Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>5+ years of technical sales or sales engineering experience in marine, clean energy, EV, aerospace, or adjacent advanced hardware industries</li> <li>Demonstrated track record of meeting or exceeding sales quotas in a complex, technical sales environment with long deal cycles</li> <li>Deep understanding of electric powertrain fundamentals — battery systems, electric motors, inverters, charging infrastructure, and thermal management — as applied to marine or EV platforms</li> <li>Proven ability to manage the full commercial cycle: prospecting, solution development, proposal writing, negotiation, and close</li> And yet it runs on technology that hasn't meaningfully changed in fifty years. Until now.</p> <p>Arc is on a mission to electrify the marine industry. We're now scaling production of our flagship model with new models and sectors on the horizon. Now we're extending our platform to commercial applications, starting with tugs, ferries, and defense, with years worth of signed contracts and a pipeline that spans every vessel type on the water.</p> <p>The technology is proven, the momentum is building, and the opportunity is enormous. The marine industry is going electric and Arc is building the platform that powers it.</p> <h2>Who we're looking for</h2> <p>We're looking for someone fired up by the mission and ready to build. You'll thrive here if you move fast, take ownership, and care about the details.</p> <p>This is a full-time, on-site role at our Los Angeles headquarters. You'll report to the Director of Software Engineering and work closely with a lean, high-trust team where everyone’s work matters.</p> <h2>What you'll do</h2> <ul> <li>Building a high-functioning team in line with Arc's values</li> <li>Providing growth opportunities and feedback to team members</li> <li>Forming technically-informed strategies to maximize impact</li> <li>Communicating with adjacent teams to improve efficiency</li> </ul> <h2>Minimum Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>6+ years of industry experience</li> <li>2+ years of management experience</li> <li>Strong grasp of the fundamentals of software engineering</li> <li>Excellent interpersonal skills in both written and verbal media</li> </ul> <h2>Bonus Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>Prior experience with Typescript and/or Python</li> <li>Prior experience with Amazon Web Services and/or Google Cloud Platform</li> <li>Prior experience with software in a hardware domain, such as embedded devices, mobile apps, IoT, and/or infotainment systems</li> <li>Comfortable pushing at a fast but sustainable pace</li> <li>Enthusiasm for boating and the water</li> </ul> <p>At Arc, we encourage diversity and consider all qualified applicants equally for employment, regardless of background, identity, or status. You're always welcome to reach out even if you don't meet many of these qualifications. Passion and aptitude make up for a lot.</p> <h2>Our values</h2> <p>We move with urgency and build with care. We value these five things above all else:</p> <ul> <li><strong>Ownership</strong> over both your work and the company's success</li> <li><strong>Empathy</strong> for colleagues and for customers</li> <li><strong>Humility</strong> in problem solving and collaboration</li> <li><strong>Pragmatism</strong> for navigating ambiguity quickly and sensibly</li> <li><strong>Inclusion</strong> of different backgrounds, opinions, cultures, and perspectives.</li> </ul> <p>We want people who share and embody these values. It is vital that our values align — we'd be happy to elaborate on any or all of these during the interview process.</p> <h2>What we offer</h2> <p>Salary M2: $200,000 M3: $220,000</p> <p>Highly competitive equity grants are included with full-time offers, as

source_jobs.title: Quality Inspector
source_jobs.description: <h2>What we're building</h2> <p>The marine industry supports recreation, global commerce, and national defense. And yet it runs on technology that hasn't meaningfully changed in fifty years. Until now.</p> <p>Arc is on a mission to electrify the marine industry. We're rethinking powertrains, software, and vessel designs from first principles. And we're already executing. We started with consumers to develop and harden our core technology. We're now scaling production of our flagship model with new models and sectors on the horizon. Now we're extending our platform to commercial applications, starting with tugs, ferries, and defense, with years worth of signed contracts and a pipeline that spans every vessel type on the water.</p> <p>The technology is proven, the momentum is building, and the opportunity is enormous. The marine industry is going electric and Arc is building the platform that powers it.</p> <h2>Who we're looking for</h2> <p>We're looking for someone fired up by the mission and ready to build. You'll thrive here if you move fast, take ownership, and care about the details.</p> <p>This is a full-time, on-site role at our Los Angeles headquarters, working first shift (6AM~3PM) with the opportunity for overtime and weekend work. You'll report to the Manager of Quality Engineering and work closely with a lean, high-trust team where everyone’s work matters.</p> <h2>What you'll do</h2> <ul> <li>Performing detailed visual and dimensional inspections of composite (carbon &amp; fiberglass) structures, metallic components, integrated boats and sub-assemblies.</li> <li>Conducting First Article, in-process, and final inspections to ensure adherence to engineering drawings and quality standards.</li> <li>Documenting inspection results and non-conformances quickly &amp; accurately in the quality management system.</li> <li>Collaborating with the production and engineering teams to identify root causes of defects and implementing corrective actions.</li> <li>Verifying that all work instructions and documentation are completed correctly before releasing parts to the next stage of production.</li> <li>Contributing to improving workshop standards, inspection protocols, and quality processes.</li> <li>Maintaining high standards of safety, quality, and output.</li> </ul> <h2>Minimum Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>Minimum of 2 years of experience as a Quality Inspector or in a comparable quality control role within a manufacturing environment.</li> <li>Proficiency using precision measuring instruments including calipers, micrometers, height gauges, and dial indicators.</li> <li>Hands-on experience inspecting composite materials, including carbon fiber and fiberglass structures, in a production or manufacturing setting.</li> <li>Ability to read and interpret engineering drawings, including geometric dimensioning and tolerancing (GD&amp;T), work instructions, and product data sheets.</li> <li>Strong attention to detail with demonstrated ability to identify cosmetic and structural defects in composite surfaces and assembled components.</li> <li>Physical ability to stand extended periods (6-10 hours per day) and lift or move materials up to 50lbs.</li> <li>Clear and effective communication skills, with the ability to document and communicate quality issues to technicians and engineers.</li> </ul> <h2>Bonus Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>Minimum of 2 years of experience as a Quality Inspector or in a comparable quality control role within a manufacturing environment.</li> <li>Proficiency using precision measuring instruments including calipers, micrometers,

source_jobs.title: Recruiting Operations Coordinator
Sales Advisor
source_jobs.description: <h2>What we're building</h2> <p>The marine industry supports recreation, global commerce, and national defense. And yet it runs on technology that hasn't meaningfully changed in fifty years. Until now.</p> <p>Arc is on a mission to electrify the marine industry. We're rethinking powertrains, software, and vessel designs from first principles. And we're already executing. We started with consumers to develop and harden our core technology. We're now scaling production of our flagship model with new models and sectors on the horizon. Now we're extending our platform to commercial applications, starting with tugs, ferries, and defense, with years worth of signed contracts and a pipeline that spans every vessel type on the water.</p> <p>The technology is proven, the momentum is building, and the opportunity is enormous. The marine industry is going electric and Arc is building the platform that powers it.</p> <h2>Who we're looking for</h2> <p>We’re looking for a proven sales closer who is fired up by our mission and ready to accelerate our growth. You’ll thrive here if you are a relentless hunter of opportunities, a master of the "soft sell" in luxury markets, and someone who takes extreme ownership of the customer journey.</p> <p>This is a full-time, on-site role at our Austin facility. You'll report to the Senior Manager of Sales and Delivery and work closely with a lean, high-trust team where everyone’s work matters.</p> <h2>What you'll do</h2> <ul> <li>Developing your pipeline and market - Proactively engaging with qualified leads and untapped opportunities to build and maintain a robust, high-conversion sales pipeline.</li> <li>Hosting Qualified Demo Experiences - Leading high-stakes, on-water demonstrations for serious prospects, expertly articulating the value proposition and performance of Arc’s electric vessels.</li> <li>Maintaining impeccable data hygiene: update deal stages, log all activities, and tag key insights so the entire go-to-market team operates on real-time facts.</li> <li>Managing Contracts: Drafting, tailoring, and executing purchase agreements, ensuring all documentation is accurate and move-ready for the customer.</li> <li>Conducting targeted outbound calls to prospects, cold leads, and unresponsive leads to generate new opportunities and expand pipeline reach.</li> <li>Identifying and presenting new opportunities for events, partnerships, and activations to continuously feed the top of the sales funnel in your market</li> <li>Facilitating Transactions: Guiding customers through the final stages of the purchase process, including financing coordination, payment milestones, and administrative requirements.</li> <li>Reporting weekly on pipeline growth, lead-source performance, and win-rate drivers—turning data into clear, actionable stories.</li> <li>Jumping into boat shows, demos, deliveries, or special events when the team needs an extra set of hands—team-first mindset, always!</li> </ul> <h2>Minimum Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>5+ years of experience in high-ticket sales (luxury automotive, marine, aviation, or premium real estate preferred) with a proven sales track record</li> <li>Strong ability to manage complex contracts and handle objections with a solution-oriented mindset.</li> <li>Enthusiasm for working with customers and educating them on new concepts</li> <li>Track record of driving towards, and exceeding, ambitious sales goals</li> <li>Experience in creating extraordinary customer experiences</li> <li>Confident boat handling skills to safely and impressively showcase vessel capabilities during demos.</li> <li>Comfortable working in a fast-pac

You'll report to the Senior Director of Operations and work closely with a lean, high-trust team where everyone’s work matters.</p> <h2>What you'll do</h2> <ul> <li>Leading a layered team of production supervisors, lead technicians, and hourly/non-exempt technicians</li> <li>Owning the production schedule end-to-end</li> <li>Proactively identifying schedule risks, capacity constraints, and resource gaps; escalating critical blockers and facilitating cross-functional resolution</li> <li>Developing short-, medium-, and long-range production plans that account for labor capacity, material availability, tooling, and facility constraints</li> <li>Partnering closely with Quality to ensure production non-conformances are identified, documented, and addressed</li> <li>Identifying and executing labor and cost-efficiency improvements</li> <li>Partnering with Facilities and EHS to maintain a safe, organized, and efficient production environment</li> <li>Identifying skill gaps on the floor and developing or procure advanced skill programs covering composite fabrication, hand assembly techniques, and quality inspection methods.</li> <li>Partnering with the People team on workforce relations matters. Developing, communicating, and enforcing team policies in a fair, consistent, and equitable manner</li> </ul> <h2>Minimum Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>Bachelor's degree or 7+ years of experience working in an aerospace, automotive, aviation, nuclear, or other manufacturing environment</li> <li>5+ years of demonstrated experience leading production in a high-quality, precision manufacturing environment in a leadership role with direct reports</li> <li>Strong technical understanding and practical knowledge of manufacturing process, systems integration, and hardware requirements</li> <li>Track record of building and executing continuous improvement programs using lean tools and structured problem-solving methodologies.</li> <li>Experience working in a startup or high-growth manufacturing environment where processes are still maturing and ambiguity is the norm.</li> </ul> <h2>Bonus Qualifications</h2> <ul> <li>Experience monitoring, tracking and continually improving total cost equation</li> <li>Composites manufacturing experience — carbon fiber layup, cure processes, prepreg handling, bonded assemblies, or sandwich structures.</li> <li>Familiarity with aerospace or defense quality management systems (AS9
